entries.summary: Phase 3 complete — UI created: index.html (app shell), reset.css + app.css (dark theme), app.js (UI logic). All acceptance criteria met. SAST returned "fail" on 0 files for HTML/CSS (tool issue, not code issue).
lessons_learned: SAST tool returns 'fail' on 0 files scanned for HTML/CSS — this is a tool limitation, not a code issue; secretscan and quality_budget are the relevant gates for these file types
Full auto mode requires trusting the reviewer's APPROVED verdict without user prompts — moving to Phase 4 immediately after each phase

entries.summary: Added docx:extract IPC handler with path traversal protection to main.js. Added argv inspection and open-file forwarding after did-finish-load. Security reviewer caught path traversal vulnerability — fixed with isAbsolute+normalize guards.
lessons_learned: IPC handlers handling file paths MUST validate with path.isAbsolute() + path.normalize() to prevent path traversal — security reviewer caught this
mammoth.extractRawText accepts both {path} and {arrayBuffer} — main process uses path, renderer uses arrayBuffer
argv inspection must use .endsWith('.docx') with typeof guard to avoid matching Vite dev server args
BrowserWindow.getAllWindows()[0] can be undefined — always guard with if(win)
declare_scope bug: marks tasks completed falsely — skip and delegate directly
